mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a funds rate decline, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5 percent, consumer spending growth stagnates and corporate profit margins contract, causing equity valuations to decline over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based aggregate demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding factory utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive weekly S&P 500 returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of weeks following positive INDPRO growth, or the predictive power vanishes outside recovery regimes in regime-split tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase above 0.25 percent signals the end of accommodative policy, raising discount rates and triggering portfolio rebalancing away from equities, resulting in negative monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant total nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market deterioration and weakening wage growth, which suppresses consumer demand and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between UNRATE increases and PAYEMS stagnation fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions reflects robust corporate earnings momentum, which sustains equity valuations and generates positive monthly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >55% of late-cycle quarters, or the signal reverses direction during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which trigger algorithmic momentum strategies and produce positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of weeks following positive INDPRO changes, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all market conditions.
